name: AD Object WriteDAC Access Detected
description: Detects the use of the WRITE_DAC permission on Active Directory objects. This permission allows modification of the discretionary access control list (DACL), which can be used to escalate privileges or maintain persistence.

prompts.prompt: A critical alert has been triggered on the Domain Controller **{hostname}**: the user **{subject_user_name}** attempted to modify the DACL (WRITE_DAC) on the Active Directory object **{object_name}**. This highly suspicious activity, with an `AccessMask` of **{access_mask}**, is a known technique for **Privilege Escalation** or **Persistence** (**MITRE ATT&CK T1222**). An adversary can use this permission to grant themselves or other accounts full control over a critical object, such as a user or group, to achieve their goals. Immediate investigation is required to determine the legitimacy of this action. This event should be treated as a confirmed security incident until proven otherwise.

parameters.name: subject_user_name
parameters.description: The user account that attempted to modify the DACL.

parameters.name: object_name
parameters.description: The name of the Active Directory object whose DACL was targeted.

parameters.name: access_mask
parameters.description: The AccessMask value associated with the event (0x40000).

parameters.name: hostname
parameters.description: The hostname of the Domain Controller where the event occurred.
